html:
There have been several individuals named Claes Fleming:

  • Claus Fleming (mentioned 1386–1427)
  • Klas Fleming (died 1597)
  • Klas Fleming (died 1616)
  • Klas Fleming (1592–1644)
  • Claes Fleming (1617–1669)
  • Claes Fleming (1649–1685)
  • Claes Fleming (1685–1766), owner of Tykö ironworks during the first half of the 18th century.
  • Claes Adolph Fleming, 1771–1831

How certain are we about this information?

From the Vasa era onwards, individuals are well-documented in archives. The uncertainty lies more in interpretation and how later generations perceived them – not least because those in power themselves played a role in shaping historical narratives.
tldr: The following is a list of several historical figures, all named Claes or Klas Fleming. It's difficult to know exactly how they lived, as old documents were often written by people in positions of power.
lattlast:
Throughout history, many individuals have borne the names Claes or Klas Fleming, living during various periods from the 14th century to the 19th century.

One of these individuals was an owner of Tykö ironworks in the early 18th century. This indicates that he owned and operated a factory or industrial complex.

We can be reasonably certain that these people existed, as they are documented in historical archives. However, it is more difficult to know precisely what their personalities were like or what they actually did, because those who recorded history were often powerful individuals who sought to portray events in a particular way.
